name: find-skills
description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
capabilities: Search for skills using the npx skills CLI
Recommend relevant skills based on user needs
Provide installation commands for discovered skills
Explain the skills ecosystem and how it works
Suggest alternative search terms when no results are found
Guide users to create custom skills when needed
author: vercel-labs
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 31 pattern matches that were all determined to be false positives. The 'external_commands' findings were markdown code blocks containing example CLI commands (npx skills), not actual code execution. The 'network' findings were documentation URLs (skills.sh). The 'weak crypto' findings were false matches on text containing 'MD' in words like 'deployment'. The skill contains only documentation with no executable code.
valueStatement: Users often do not know which skills exist to help with their tasks. This skill guides them to discover relevant capabilities from the open agent ecosystem and install them with simple commands.
limitations: Cannot browse the skills registry directly without using the CLI
Skill recommendations are based on keyword matching, not semantic understanding
Installation requires user confirmation or explicit permission flags
Only discovers skills from the public skills ecosystem

useCases.title: Find Performance Optimization Skills
useCases.description: A developer asks how to make their React app faster. The skill searches for and recommends the vercel-react-best-practices skill with installation instructions.

useCases.title: Discover PR Review Tools
useCases.description: A team lead wants help reviewing pull requests. The skill finds PR review skills and explains how to install them for automated code review assistance.

useCases.title: Locate Documentation Generators
useCases.description: A maintainer needs to create a changelog. The skill searches for documentation-related skills and provides options for changelog generation.

promptTemplates.title: Search for a Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Find me a skill for [task description]
promptTemplates.scenario: When the user needs a specific capability

promptTemplates.title: Explore by Category
promptTemplates.prompt: What skills exist for [category like testing, deployment, design]?
promptTemplates.scenario: When the user wants to browse available tools

promptTemplates.title: Get Skill Help
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use the skills CLI to [find/add/update] skills?
promptTemplates.scenario: When the user needs help with skill management

bestPractices: Use specific keywords when searching - 'react testing' works better than just 'testing'
Try alternative terms if the first search does not return relevant results
Check skills from verified sources like vercel-labs for quality assurance
antiPatterns: Do not install skills without reviewing what they do first
Avoid vague queries like 'help me' - use specific task descriptions
Do not assume a skill exists for every niche task

faq.question: What is the Skills CLI?
faq.answer: The Skills CLI (npx skills) is the package manager for the open agent skills ecosystem. It allows you to search for, install, and manage skills that extend AI agent capabilities.

faq.question: How do I install a skill?
faq.answer: Use the command 'npx skills add <owner/repo@skill>'. Add the -g flag for global installation and -y to skip confirmation prompts.

faq.question: Where can I browse available skills?
faq.answer: Visit https://skills.sh/ to browse the skills registry, or use 'npx skills find [query]' to search from the command line.

faq.question: What if no skill exists for my task?
faq.answer: If no skill is found, the AI can still help directly using general capabilities. You can also create your own skill with 'npx skills init <name>'.

faq.question: Are skills safe to install?
faq.answer: Skills come from various sources. Review the skill documentation and source repository before installing. Skills from verified organizations like vercel-labs are generally trustworthy.

faq.question: How do I update installed skills?
faq.answer: Run 'npx skills check' to check for updates, and 'npx skills update' to update all installed skills to their latest versions.
